behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the module handoff between source collectors and the summary-input assembler (cross-module wiring/state merge). Unit is too low because this is not one function’s isolated logic; it is about combined outputs being carried forward. System was considered and rejected because behavior text only requires inclusion of data, not real network/OS effects from the sources. Agentic/Workflow were rejected because this behavior stops before evaluating model decisions; concrete disqualifier: it checks "content passed to the summary-generation step," not summary quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collector/assembler modules together with three deterministic in-process source adapters (metrics, tracker, deploy log), run collection once, and assert the exact payload sent to summary generation contains all three sections with correct provenance markers.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at error-propagation boundaries across modules: source client failure -> report assembly fallback note -> pipeline continues. Unit is too low because it requires interaction between source adapter, orchestration, and renderer. System was considered and rejected: behavior text says "one data source is unavailable" but does not require reproducing real network/kernel failure modes; an in-process failing adapter can expose the intended fallback bug. Agentic/Workflow rejected because correctness is fallback orchestration, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestration with two normal source adapters and one adapter that raises timeout/unavailable errors; assert pipeline completes, output includes a `data unavailable` marker only for that section, and no global abort flag/error exit is emitted.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model output quality (factual faithfulness to key values), which a stub would always hide. Integration is insufficient because deterministic fixtures cannot verify whether the model decides/writes correctly. Workflow was considered and rejected because behavior text specifies one summary output check, not multiple agentic decisions chained across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summary model calls on a fixed evaluation set with known key values; score factual consistency (e.g., exact-value inclusion/contradiction checks) across repeated runs and fail if accuracy drops below threshold or hallucinated key metrics appear.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end with real external delivery, but execution also passes through real summary generation in the full pipeline; mocking the model can hide real failures caused by real model outputs in final email content/format. So System alone is not enough as minimum for this behavior’s full-path claim. Workflow was considered and rejected because only one model decision point is required (single summary-generation step), not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scheduled pipeline against live/staging dependencies, including real summary-generation call and real mail delivery path; assert recipient inbox receives one report email with expected sections and valid rendered narrative/body structure for that run.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real infrastructure boundaries: unreachable email service, local file persistence, and next scheduled run retry timing. In-process mocks can hide filesystem/path/permission and scheduler/process interaction failures. Agentic was considered and rejected because correctness here is delivery fallback/retry mechanics; concrete disqualifier: behavior text focuses on "saved to a local file" and "retried on the next scheduled run," not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduler job with mail endpoint intentionally unreachable, verify report artifact is actually written to disk at configured path, then trigger next scheduled run with mail restored and assert queued artifact is delivered and backlog state is cleared.
